title: dendrocnide torricellensis
intro:
Dendrocnide torricellensis, also known as the giant stinging tree, is a species of tree native to Papua New Guinea, characterized by its highly toxic sap that contains urushiol, a chemical compound responsible for its severe skin irritation effects.

The medicinal properties of Dendrocnide torricellensis are derived from its sap, which has been traditionally used in local medicine to treat various ailments. However, its potent toxicity limits its use and requires careful handling.

While the health benefits of the plant are not well-documented, its sap is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and infection prevention. 

Due to its toxicity and limited availability, Dendrocnide torricellensis is not typically used in herbal preparations and is instead handled and used by local practitioners with caution.
